pairs.label: SPY vs Dollar
pairs.subtitle: Risk-on/off pair — weak but persistent inverse; dollar strengthens as equities sell off into safe-haven flows

pairs.label: SPY vs Crude Oil
pairs.subtitle: Demand-driven pair — both fall on weak growth expectations; inverts on geopolitical supply shocks

pairs.label: SPY vs Gold
pairs.subtitle: Mildly positive long-run pair — both are real assets; turns sharply inverse in crises as gold becomes a safe haven while equities fall

pairs.label: SPY vs Long Bonds
pairs.subtitle: Historically inverse (flight-to-safety pre-2022) — both fell together during the 2022 rate shock, making this pair the most regime-sensitive on the board

pairs.label: Dollar vs Gold
pairs.subtitle: Historically robust inverse — gold priced in USD globally; strong dollar raises cost for foreign buyers

pairs.label: Dollar vs EM
pairs.subtitle: Strong dollar = higher EM debt servicing costs and capital outflows; one of the more reliable inverse pairs

pairs.label: Dollar vs Commodities
pairs.subtitle: Theoretical inverse (USD-priced commodities) — but relationship has been unreliable since 2016 and near-random post-COVID

pairs.label: Discretionary vs Staples
pairs.subtitle: Economic cycle gauge — co-move in bull markets; staples outperform in downturns as capital rotates to defensive
